title: electra mutica var. simplicifolia
intro: Electra mutica var. simplicifolia, also known as the Siberian fleabane or Elecampane, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family. The plant's roots, rhizomes, and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Elecampane include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots can be made into a tincture, decoction, or dried herb for use in teas, infusions, and topical applications.
